LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MAMA MIA PIZZERIA, ARTEM EMMANUILOV AND AGABIE EMMANUILOV'S MOTION FOR ORDER SETTING ASIDE AND VACATING DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND GRANTING DEFENDANTS LEAVE TO DEFEND IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT COMPLIED WITH LABOR CODE 98(F) PRIOR TO FILING THIS MOTION. IN HIS LETTER DATED FEBRUARY 25, 2013, DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL REQUESTED A STAY OF EXECUTION, WHICH THE LABOR COMMISSIONER DENIED. (REDMOND DECL., EXHS. K, L.) DEFENDANTS DID NOT APPLY FOR RELIEF PURSUANT LABOR CODE 98(F). "'[T]HE WORD "RELIEF" [IN THE LAST SENTENCE OF SECTION 98(F)] CLEARLY REFERS TO RELIEF FROM SUCH FAILURE TO EITHER ANSWER OR APPEAR' IN THE ADMINISTRATIVE PROCEEDING." (GONZALEZ V. BECK (2007) 158 CAL.APP.4TH 598, 605.) FURTHER, "[I]T IS APPARENT FROM THE LANGUAGE OF SECTION 98(F) THAT THE LEGISLATURE INTENDED TO REQUIRE EMPLOYERS WHO DO NOT ANSWER OR APPEAR IN AN ADMINISTRATIVE WAGE CLAIM PROCEEDING, AND WHO THEREAFTER DO NOT TIMELY APPEAL FROM A DECISION OF THE COMMISSIONER, TO RESORT TO THE SPECIFIC ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDY FOR RELIEF IN ACCORDANCE WITH CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 473 AS A PREREQUISITE TO JUDICIAL ACTION." ID. AT 607. THE LABOR COMMISSIONER'S DENIAL OF A STAY OF EXECUTION DID NOT RENDER DEFENDANTS' EXHAUSTION OF ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES FUTILE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Setting Aside And Vacating Default And Default Judgment And Granting Defts Leave To Defend
Off Calendar May-24-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was uncontested. DEFENDANTS MAMA MIA PIZZERIA, ARTEM EMMANUILOV and AGABIE EMMANUILOV's Motion For Order Setting Aside And Vacating Default And Default Judgment And Granting Defendants Leave To Defend is denied without prejudice. Defendants have not complied with Labor Code 98(f) prior to filing this motion. In his letter dated February 25, 2013, Defendants' counsel requested a stay of execution, which the Labor Commissioner denied. (Redmond Decl., Exhs. K, L.) Defendants did not apply for relief pursuant Labor Code 98(f). "'[T]he word "relief" [in the last sentence of section 98(f)] clearly refers to relief from such failure to either answer or appear' in the administrative proceeding." (Gonzalez v. Beck (2007) 158 Cal.App.4th 598, 605.) Further, "[i]t is apparent from the language of section 98(f) that the Legislature intended to require employers who do not answer or appear in an administrative wage claim proceeding, and who thereafter do not timely appeal from a decision of the Commissioner, to resort to the specific administrative remedy for relief in accordance with Code of Civil Procedure section 473 as a prerequisite to judicial action." Id. at 607. The Labor Commissioner's denial of a stay of execution did not render Defendants' exhaustion of administrative remedies futile. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Setting Aside And Vacating Default And Default Judgment And Granting Defts Leave To Defend

MINI MINUTES:

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Setting Aside And Vacating Default And Default Judgment And Granting Defts Leave To Defend
Off Calendar Apr-10-2013 Continued To May-10-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 10, 2013, Line 8, DEFENDANTS MAMA MIA PIZZERIA, ARTEM EMMANUILOV, AGABIE EMMANUILOV'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Setting Aside And Vacating Default And Default Judgment And Granting Defts Leave To Defend is continued on the court's own motion to May 10, 2013 to give Defendants the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Defendants must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than April 10, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. The court has courtesy copies of the opposition and reply papers. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

Hearing On Defendant'S Claim Of Exemption
Off Calendar Jan-16-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Arkady Itkin, Esq. of the Law Offices of Arkady Itkin, Tel. 415.640.6765, specially appearing for claimant Agabie Emmanuilov, who is also present in court;
Fred Keeperman, Esq. of the Law Offices of Fred Keeperman, Tel. 925.631.7710, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT AGABIE EMMANUILOV'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION is denied. Agabie Emmanuilov is a judgment debtor. Claimant has not provided proof that claimant has any legal basis for an exemption. Court to prepare and serve the order. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
